delta.text: `luaK_reserveregs(fs, 1)` call is commented out as a TODO, so the table's register at `fs.freereg` is never
actually reserved. The expression descriptor `t` claims `info=0` (N

content.text:
Found the bug. In `constructor` (lib.rs:2355), the `luaK_reserveregs(fs, 1)` call is commented out as a TODO, so the table's register at `fs.freereg` is never actually reserved. The expression descriptor `t` claims `info=0` (NonReloc), but `fs.freereg` stays at 0. Then `adjust_assign` → `cg_exp_to_next_reg` → `cg_free_exp` → `cg_free_reg(0)` triggers `debug_assert_eq!(0, 0-1)`.

Let me apply the fix.

delta.partial_json: ", "old_string": "    let freereg = ls.fs.as_ref().unwrap().freereg as i32;\n    // C: init_exp(t, VNON
RELOC, fs->freereg); luaK_reserveregs(fs, 1)\n    init_exp(t, ExprKind::NonReloc, freereg);
", "new_string": "    let freereg = ls.fs.as_ref().unwrap().freereg as i32;\n    // C: init_exp(t, VNONRELOC, fs->freereg); luaK_reserve

delta.text: The fix worked — the test now passes all 4 steps. No temporary eprintln!() were added, so nothing to clean up.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2355` in `constructor()`. The C code does `init_exp(t, VN
ONRELOC, fs->freereg); luaK_reserveregs(fs, 1);` but the Rust port left the `reserve_regs` call commented out as `//
TODO(port): lua_code::reserve_regs(...)`. So the table constructor expression claimed `e.u.
info = 0` (NonReloc, register 0) while `fs.freereg` stayed at 0. Later in `adjust_ass

_eq!(reg, fs.freereg - 1)` fired with `0 != -1`.

**Fix:** Replaced the T
ODO comment with an actual call to the existing local helper: `reserve_regs(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap(

), 1)?;` (one line). The `reserve_regs` function was already defined at line 610.

**New error:** None — the full pipeline now succeeds
: `[1/4]…[4/4] Executing chunk... [ok] execution completed, status=Ok`. The test program

content.text:
The fix worked — the test now passes all 4 steps. No temporary eprintln!() were added, so nothing to clean up.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2355` in `constructor()`. The C code does `init_exp(t, VNONRELOC, fs->freereg); luaK_reserveregs(fs, 1);` but the Rust port left the `reserve_regs` call commented out as `// TODO(port): lua_code::reserve_regs(...)`. So the table constructor expression claimed `e.u.info = 0` (NonReloc, register 0) while `fs.freereg` stayed at 0. Later in `adjust_assign` → `cg_exp_to_next_reg` → `cg_free_exp` → `cg_free_reg(0)`, the LIFO assertion `debug_assert_eq!(reg, fs.freereg - 1)` fired with `0 != -1`.

**Fix:** Replaced the TODO comment with an actual call to the existing local helper: `reserve_regs(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap(), 1)?;` (one line). The `reserve_regs` function was already defined at line 610.

**New error:** None — the full pipeline now succeeds: `[1/4]…[4/4] Executing chunk... [ok] execution completed, status=Ok`. The test program `local t = {10,20,30}; print(t[1])` runs cleanly end-to-end.

result:
The fix worked — the test now passes all 4 steps. No temporary eprintln!() were added, so nothing to clean up.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2355` in `constructor()`. The C code does `init_exp(t, VNONRELOC, fs->freereg); luaK_reserveregs(fs, 1);` but the Rust port left the `reserve_regs` call commented out as `// TODO(port): lua_code::reserve_regs(...)`. So the table constructor expression claimed `e.u.info = 0` (NonReloc, register 0) while `fs.freereg` stayed at 0. Later in `adjust_assign` → `cg_exp_to_next_reg` → `cg_free_exp` → `cg_free_reg(0)`, the LIFO assertion `debug_assert_eq!(reg, fs.freereg - 1)` fired with `0 != -1`.

**Fix:** Replaced the TODO comment with an actual call to the existing local helper: `reserve_regs(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap(), 1)?;` (one line). The `reserve_regs` function was already defined at line 610.

**New error:** None — the full pipeline now succeeds: `[1/4]…[4/4] Executing chunk... [ok] execution completed, status=Ok`. The test program `local t = {10,20,30}; print(t[1])` runs cleanly end-to-end.
